title: Xerisced Buttery Spanish Lamb Shoulder Cocido Madrileno
description: Cocido Madrileno is a hearty, comforting Spanish stew originating from Madrid. This recipe features tender lamb shoulder cooked in a rich, buttery broth with a variety of vegetables and chickpeas.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, onion, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the chickpeas, chickpea broth, water, tomato paste, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours.
Add the butter and stir until melted.
Stir in the parsley, lemon juice, and chili flakes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Traditionally, Cocido Madrileno is served with crusty bread and a side of alioli.
For a more authentic experience, serve with a side of chickpeas and vegetables.
To make the alioli, combine 1/2 cup of mayonnaise, 2 cloves of minced garlic, and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice in a bowl. Mix well and refrigerate until ready to serve.
To make the chickpeas, cook 1 cup of chickpeas in boiling water until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and serve with a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve the lamb shoulder with a side of chickpeas and vegetables, and a dollop of alioli on top.
Alternatively, serve the lamb shoulder on a bed of chickpeas and vegetables, with a side of bread for dipping in the broth.
To make the broth more flavorful, add 1/4 cup of chopped fresh parsley and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice to the pot during the last 30 minutes of cooking.
For a vegetarian version of the recipe, substitute the lamb shoulder with 1 cup of sliced portobello mushrooms.
